sn46.3:1.1: “Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who are accomplished in ethical conduct, accomplished in *samādhi*, accomplished in knowledge, accomplished in liberation, accomplished in the knowledge and vision of liberation — I say, *bhikkhus*, that even seeing those *bhikkhus* is very helpful;
sn46.3:1.2: I say, *bhikkhus*, that even hearing those *bhikkhus* is very helpful;
sn46.3:1.3: I say, *bhikkhus*, that even approaching those *bhikkhus* is very helpful;
sn46.3:1.4: I say, *bhikkhus*, that even attending on those *bhikkhus* is very helpful;
sn46.3:1.5: I say, *bhikkhus*, that even recollecting those *bhikkhus* is very helpful;
sn46.3:1.6: I say, *bhikkhus*, that even going forth after those *bhikkhus* is very helpful.
sn46.3:1.8: Having heard the *dhamma* of such *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, one dwells withdrawn with twofold withdrawal — with bodily withdrawal and with withdrawal of mind [*cittavūpakāsena*].
sn46.3:1.9: Dwelling withdrawn in that way, he recollects and thinks over that *dhamma*.
sn46.3:2.1: At the time, *bhikkhus*, when a *bhikkhu*, dwelling withdrawn in that way, recollects and thinks over that *dhamma*, the *sati* *bojjhaṅga* [*satisambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu* at that time;
sn46.3:2.2: at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *sati* *bojjhaṅga* [*satisambojjhaṅgaṁ*];
sn46.3:2.3: at that time the *sati* *bojjhaṅga* [*satisambojjhaṅgo*] goes to fulfillment through development in that *bhikkhu*.
sn46.3:2.4: Dwelling with *sati* in that way, he investigates that *dhamma* with discernment [*paññāya*], examines it, and enters upon inquiry into it.
sn46.3:3.1: At the time, *bhikkhus*, when a *bhikkhu*, dwelling with *sati* in that way, investigates that *dhamma* with discernment [*paññāya*], examines it, and enters upon inquiry into it, the *dhamma*-investigation *bojjhaṅga* [*dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu* at that time;
sn46.3:3.2: at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *dhamma*-investigation *bojjhaṅga* [*dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgaṁ*];
sn46.3:3.3: at that time the *dhamma*-investigation *bojjhaṅga* [*dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo*] goes to fulfillment through development in that *bhikkhu*.
sn46.3:3.4: As he investigates that *dhamma* with discernment [*paññāya*], examines it, and enters upon inquiry into it, energy is aroused in him, not slack.
sn46.3:4.1: At the time, *bhikkhus*, when energy is aroused and not slack in a *bhikkhu* as he investigates that *dhamma* with discernment [*paññāya*], examines it, and enters upon inquiry into it, the energy *bojjhaṅga* [*vīriyasambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu* at that time; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the energy *bojjhaṅga* [*vīriyasambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the energy *bojjhaṅga* [*vīriyasambojjhaṅgo*] goes to fulfillment through development in that *bhikkhu*.
sn46.3:4.2: In one whose energy is aroused, unworldly *pīti* arises.
sn46.3:5.1: At the time, *bhikkhus*, when unworldly *pīti* arises in a *bhikkhu* whose energy is aroused, the *pīti* *bojjhaṅga* [*pītisambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu* at that time; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *pīti* *bojjhaṅga* [*pītisambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the *pīti* *bojjhaṅga* [*pītisambojjhaṅgo*] goes to fulfillment through development in that *bhikkhu*.
sn46.3:5.2: For one whose mind is filled with *pīti* [*pītimanassa*], the body becomes tranquil, and the mind [*cittaṁ*] becomes tranquil.
sn46.3:6.1: At the time, *bhikkhus*, when, in a *bhikkhu* whose mind is filled with *pīti* [*pītimanassa*], the body becomes tranquil and the mind [*cittaṁ*] becomes tranquil, the tranquility *bojjhaṅga* [*passaddhisambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu* at that time; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the tranquility *bojjhaṅga* [*passaddhisambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the tranquility *bojjhaṅga* [*passaddhisambojjhaṅgo*] goes to fulfillment through development in that *bhikkhu*.
sn46.3:6.2: For one whose body is tranquil and who is at ease, the mind [*cittaṁ*] becomes endowed with *samādhi*.
sn46.3:7.1: At the time, *bhikkhus*, when the mind [*cittaṁ*] becomes endowed with *samādhi* in a *bhikkhu* whose body is tranquil and who is at ease, the *samādhi* *bojjhaṅga* [*samādhisambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu* at that time; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *samādhi* *bojjhaṅga* [*samādhisambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the *samādhi* *bojjhaṅga* [*samādhisambojjhaṅgo*] goes to fulfillment through development in that *bhikkhu*.
sn46.3:7.2: He becomes one who carefully looks on with equanimity [*upekkhā*] at the mind [*cittaṁ*] thus endowed with *samādhi* [*tathāsamāhitaṁ*].
sn46.3:8.1: At the time, *bhikkhus*, when a *bhikkhu* becomes one who carefully looks on with equanimity [*upekkhā*] at the mind [*cittaṁ*] thus endowed with *samādhi* [*tathāsamāhitaṁ*], the equanimity *bojjhaṅga* [*upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu* at that time; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the equanimity *bojjhaṅga* [*upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the equanimity *bojjhaṅga* [*upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo*] goes to fulfillment through development in that *bhikkhu*.
sn46.3:9.1: When the seven *sambojjhaṅgas* have been developed in this way, *bhikkhus*, and cultivated in this way, seven fruits and seven benefits may be expected.
sn46.3:9.10: When the seven *bojjhaṅgas* have been developed in this way, *bhikkhus*, and cultivated in this way, these seven fruits and seven benefits may be expected.”
sn46.3:9.2: What seven fruits and seven benefits?
sn46.3:9.3: In this very life he attains final knowledge early.
sn46.3:9.4: If he does not attain final knowledge early in this very life, then he attains final knowledge at the time of death.
sn46.3:9.5: If he does not attain final knowledge early in this very life, and if he does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, then, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who attains final *nibbāna* in between.
sn46.3:9.6: If he does not attain final knowledge early in this very life, and if he does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* in between, then, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who attains final *nibbāna* on landing.
sn46.3:9.7: If he does not attain final knowledge early in this very life, and if he does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* in between, and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* on landing, then, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who attains final *nibbāna* without *saṅkhāras* [*asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*].
sn46.3:9.8: If he does not attain final knowledge early in this very life, and if he does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* in between, and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* on landing, and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* without *saṅkhāras* [*asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*], then, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who attains final *nibbāna* with *saṅkhāras* [*sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*].
sn46.3:9.9: If he does not attain final knowledge early in this very life, and if he does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* in between, and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* on landing, and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* without *saṅkhāras* [*asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*], and if, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he does not become one who attains final *nibbāna* with *saṅkhāras* [*sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*], then, with the destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one going upstream, bound for the Akaniṭṭha realm.
